marketState: neutral, leaning cautiously bullish near-term — hot 8-16D demand spike and extreme-short COT positioning offset by a still-bearish daily/weekly technical trend
summary: Seasonality (vsMedian, probHigher, typicalMove, reliability), the fair-value band at all horizons, and storage are all null in this snapshot, so the two highest-priority inputs for gauging value are unavailable and conviction must stay low. What is usable is bullish for near-term demand: the 8-16 day weather window is running +29.5% above the 5-year norm with rangePos above 1.0 (aboveMax), while the 1-7 day window is a milder +8.4% above normal — classic mid-to-late-summer cooling-demand strength. Managed-money futures positioning is net short (-105,709 contracts) at just the 8th percentile of its 5-year range, an unusually stretched short base that raises squeeze risk if heat persists. Technicals are split by timeframe: intraday (5m/15m/1h) are bullish-to-neutral with RSI in the high-50s/low-60s, but 4h/1d/1w trend read bearish with daily/weekly RSI at 37/37 and EMA50<EMA200 — the dominant higher-timeframe trend has not turned. The futures curve shows only a sliver of backwardation front-to-next (-$0.018) before building into a large, normal winter contango (Jan27 +$1.28 vs front), consistent with typical injection-season-to-withdrawal-season carry rather than a special signal.

longTerm.rationale: Three of the five long-term priority inputs — seasonality (vsMedian/probHigher/typicalMove), the fair-value band, and storage vs-5y/surprise — are null in this snapshot, so there is no reliable valuation anchor for a multi-week position. The only usable long-horizon signals (elevated 8-16D weather demand and an extreme-short, 8th-percentile MM COT position) both lean bullish, but that is not enough on its own to size a 3-12 week trade responsibly.
longTerm.trigger: Wait for seasonality (vsMedian/probHigher/reliability), the fair-value band, and storage vs-5y data to populate. If they confirm price at/below fair value with room per seasonality and storage is not oversupplied, that supports acting on the existing bullish weather/COT skew; if fair value shows price already rich, stand aside despite the weather/COT tilt.

shortTerm.rationale: The fair-value band (now/plus7D) needed to judge whether $2.886 front is cheap or rich is null, and technicals disagree by timeframe — intraday leans bullish (5m trend bullish, RSI ~60) while 4h/1d trend bearish with MACD histogram negative on 5m/1d — so there is no clean technical or valuation edge to trade over the next 1-10 days despite the supportive 1-7D weather demand reading (+8.4% vs 5y, rangePos 0.68, not extreme).
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either (a) fair-value band data to return showing front trading at/below the fair-value floor, giving a defined low-risk long zone, or (b) technical alignment across 1h/4h/1d (e.g. daily RSI reclaiming >45 with a bullish EMA cross) to confirm the intraday bullish tilt is spreading to higher timeframes before entering.
